reasoning: The recent 2025-08 value of the 1-year expected inflation rate (around 2.59 to 2.69) is slightly above the 12-month historical mean (approx 2.53%). High month-to-month variation is present, leading to a strong mean-reversion tendency. Since the starting point is above the average, a subsequent drop is slightly more likely than an increase in the short term. The FRED statistical analysis gives a 47% combined probability of exceeding the threshold for the 1-month forecast horizon. I have slightly adjusted this down to reflect the likely reversion to the mean from elevated levels, with estimates progressively rising back to 0.49 for the longest timeline to represent increasing uncertainty.
